entry.title: Re: Using SAP CAP with IntelliJ
entry.content:
Exciting news: SAP CDS Language Support for IntelliJ is now available in the JetBrains Marketplace!
Just head over there and click the "Install to …" button to get started, or open the plugin settings in your IDE and search for "SAP CDS Language Support".

Note that the plugin is only compatible with the paid IntelliJ IDEs, such as IDEA Ultimate and WebStorm.

For the full readme, see https://github.com/cap-js/cds-intellij.

entry.title: Re: Planning Segments in Monitor Material Coverage profil...
entry.content:
Got the answer - SAP help 

Abbreviation Technical Key MRP Segment Description

NETPLA02

Net requirements segment

Consists of that part of the list for which the net requirements calculation in planning was carried out on plant level.

BRTPLA04

Gross requirements segment

The gross segment contains the MRP elements from gross requirements planning.

KNDPLA20

Individual customer segment

An individual customer segment is created per individual customer order for make-to-order production.

PROPLA22

Individual project segment

An individual project segment is created per project for project planning.

MSTPLA24

Segment for direct production or direct procurement

Segment is created for materials that are directly produced or procured without planning.

VORPLA30

Planning segment/ no final assembly

This segment groups together the MRP elements which are planned without final assembly, as yet. Once the sales order arrives the final assembly is initiated and is consumed with the planned requirements.

MSPLA32

Planning Segment for direct production or direct procurement

Segment is created for the planning of materials that are directly produced or procured.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content: Please find below my submission of week 4

entry.title: Involving Customer Feedback: New Outage Experience in SAP for Me
entry.content:
In today's 24/7 business environment, time is an organization's most valuable resource, especially when dealing with outages that require immediate resolution to keep systems and applications running. To streamline decision-making during such events and reduce solution time, SAP collaborated with four customers to develop a new Outage Experience within SAP for Me, the personalized central entry point providing quick access to SAP products, support, and services.

 

Actively involving customers

With the SAP Fiori Makers, SAP offers customer bootcamps to design solutions for specific use cases. Recently, adidas, Boehringer Ingelheim, Endress+Hauser, and BASF participated in a four-day bootcamp, gaining valuable insights into SAP Fiori and their end-user needs.

Customer Sascha Dörner, Head of SAP Cross Applications at Boehringer Ingelheim, highlights the success of these bootcamps and his user experience in a positive vein: “We were extremely surprised on how well the finished solution met our expectations and requirements. The methodology and implementation of the bootcamp deserves a special mention, as both played a key role in ensuring that the solution includes end-user feedback and brings real added value. I came to the workshop with a precise idea of the problem and how it could be solved. The professional approach opened further perspectives and dimensions for us, which ultimately led to an even better result.”

Supporting this view, Judith Schneider, UX Designer and SAP Fiori expert, describes the bootcamp setup and goals stating, “We include customers and users from the very beginning of our development journey. In doing so, new ideas flow and areas for improvement can be identified early on. Following this design-led development approach, our customers help shape the user experience according to their expectations.”

 

SAP’s design-led development process guarantees a solid and consistent user experience that addresses the needs of end-users across all lines of business, tasks, and devices.

To ensure that the outage solution will meet end-user needs, the main persona—an IT admin—has been defined and SAP’s Scenes tool has been used to visualize the user story during an outage. This collaborative process identified additional personas, key notifications, and communication channels, leading participants to create low-fi mockups and ultimately translate the use case into SAP Fiori for a high-fi mockup and a future software prototype.

With Scenes, storyboards about products and services can be created fast, collaboratively, and iteratively.

Benefits of the new Outage Experience in SAP for Me

The new Outage Experience in SAP for Me ensures that users stay informed with real-time event handling across mobile apps and smartwatches. Nathalie Pardini, SAP Product Owner, emphasizes, “This innovative solution gives customers the ability to customize outage notifications according to their specific business needs. By targeting specific users, businesses can ensure that the right people receive important outage information in a timely manner. Thanks to the valuable input from our representative customers, this solution has been widely embraced since its launch and continues to gain momentum.”

The new feature, Bundles, is a flexible grouping of cloud services and systems that offers three key benefits to SAP customers:

  • Customers can give intuitive names to Bundles and mark them with a business criticality, allowing for customized and prioritized notification management.
  • Notifications provide clear and accessible information about the specific parts of their business and processes affected by downtime, enabling customers to quickly understand the impact and take appropriate action.
  • Only relevant and impacted key users will be notified thanks to subscriptions on Bundles, ensuring that the right individuals receive timely and essential information. A notification history provides ongoing updates and post-incident summaries, complete with root cause analysis, helping prevent future outages.

Martin Buselmeier, Solution Architect Integration and Platform at Endress+Hauser, highlights the way this new solution meets their organization’s expectations: “Bundles are the perfect opportunity for us to group various SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP services that relate in various ways. By freely choosing the names, we can use Endress+Hauser's own terminology for recognition and quickly establish the context. Through the subscription, it is now also possible for us to automatically inform stakeholders outside SAP for Me, such as key users or project managers, about downtimes without having to involve an interpreter.”

The new Outage Experience now offers all the user-desired information to handle future outages most efficiently.

Optimizing products with user research – be part of it!

Engaging customers and end-users with their feedback early in the development of the Outage Experience was pivotal in creating a solution that genuinely met their needs. By gaining both a deep understanding of their pain points and needs, SAP successfully developed a product that resonated with users. As one end-user noted, "This is a very user-friendly and functional solution that truly helps customers adapt to specific scenarios or events."

This underscores the critical role user research plays in driving successful product development. In 2023, SAP introduced the SAP User Research Panel, a user research initiative that invites end-users to participate in remote research activities. They can share their product and design experiences, help shape SAP products to better align with their needs, while also offering them a preview of new features or products.


Make your voice heard! Join the SAP User Research Panel. Registration is unbinding and easy, taking part in research studies is convenient, taking ten to sixty minutes.
To learn more about the SAP Fiori Makers bootcamp offering, which includes a few days long physical workshop following the design-led development process, please visit their webpage.
Are you interested in more info about the Outage Experience and Bundles feature? Explore more in Nathalie’s most recent blog. For more information about SAP for Me, have a look here.

This article originally appeared on SAPinsider.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content: Done

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI - How to create one summary log file and send...
entry.content: Hello Karthik, I would advise you to write log to data store.  Then you can create a separate flow to read logs from data store and email them to business owner. Data store will allow you to keep your logs even in the case if email delivery fails. You can trigger it or manually download the log from data store at a later time.

entry.title: Re: Comparison of SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (SAC edition) vs. SAC add-in for Microsoft Excel
entry.content: @LavanyaJ Yes, Classic Account Model is still supported in AFO. As SAC recommends to use the New Model I didn't mention it explicitly anymore. Same counts for Datasphere Datasets.

entry.title: Re: Simplifying Data Navigation: Leveraging the SAP BW Report-Report Interface in SAP Analytics Clou

Help.

Does SAC support jump targets for Datasphere Analytic Models? Like the BW Live connections described above.

You want the ability to pass a filter from 1 SAC Story with a Datasphere Analytic Model to a 2nd Story/Datasphere Analytic Model. 

I have tried the hyperlinks with dimension filters, but this does not filter the target stories data set.

entry.title: How to count non-unique names from a list in SAP Story
entry.content:
How to create a calculated column in a database which returns "Unique" if user ID appears once & "Not Unique" if it appears more than once?

I have a database with a list of user IDs. Here, a user ID may be unique or may be repeated. Is there a way I can add a calculated column to my query which returns "Unique" for those user IDs that appear once in the database and "Not Unique" for those user IDs that appear two or more times?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
Okay. Let's sum up. Please confirm if it's okay or not.

Currently, one user plugs a dongle on his/her laptop, then runs an ABAP program that you have created, which runs some ActiveX code via OLE (CREATE OBJECT, CALL METHOD, etc.) on the user's laptop, and it succeeds.

Now, what is your exact requirement? (and why "background")

entry.title: SAP IBP - RTI; Navigation to other Systems
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
in Order Based Planning / Real Time Integration it is possible to integrate several external systems in one planning area.

So that the system can distinguish the product ID, IBP adds the logical system to the product ID.

See:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_INTEGRATED_BUSINESS_PLANNING/68fa1e86fe6f41d98421d1ce13a08a9f/fcfae20979cb421183e092513d1a4128.html?locale=en-US

Example:
Prd-ID S4: FG126
Prd-ID IBP: FG126@COE

This link can be seen in the app: “Product Mappings - Real Time Integration”.

Now to my question:
I would like to jump into the S4 system via the Planner Workspace. There are also best practice instructions for configuring this.

When I implement these, the system jumps to the source system, but opens the selected app with the IBP product ID (FG126@COE) and not the product ID of the source system.

How can I change this so that the system selects directly with the correct product ID?

I have tried to change this with the help of an additional attribute in the planning area and by changing the source attribute in the Dynamic Attributes section in the Manage Navigation to Other Systems app. However, this means that the navigation option is no longer displayed in the Planner workspace. If I undo the change in PRDID, the navigation option is immediately available again.

Many thanks for your support

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge
entry.content:
In your metadata extension file, annotate your CDS view with @ui.facet and specify the required properties. Facets organize information on an object page. 

purpose:#STANDARD
type:#IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE

entry.title: Re: Domestic Revue GL should be Changed to Export Revenue...
entry.content:
Scenario 1 - Cancel original invoice
1) Once accounting effect is generated then SAP not allow us to
change so in that case we need to cancel original invoice.
2) After cancelling the invoice SAP generates a reverse
accounting effect of original invoice
3) Before creating a new invoice need to change in configuration part
as you know SAP not assign/define GL automatically against the
invoice some contra configuration happened in your case.
4) After change the configuration create new invoice

Scenario 2 - without cancel original invoice
If not possible to cancel invoice due to the legel or
any other reason in that case below is the option
1) Use FS00 t code and open GL account remove check box "Post automatically only"
2) Now you are free to enter journal voucher entry to settle the account
if account department okay
3) Again fill check box "Post automatically only" in FS00 t code

As per my knowledge Credit note is not a solution
if you generate a document with reference of the original inovice.

If you create a credit note without using a reference document in
that case you need to configure a credit note accounting properly.

Please inform if you require to change in configuration for invoice
or credit note.

Hope above solutions are works for you.

entry.title: Re: Domestic Revue GL should be Changed to Export Revenue GL after Buyon certificate submission in V
entry.content: Scenario 1 - Cancel original invoice 1) Once accounting effect is generated then SAP not allow us to change so in that case we need to cancel original invoice. 2) After cancelling the invoice SAP generates a reverse accounting effect of original invoice 3) Before creating a new invoice need to change in configuration part as you know SAP not assign/define GL automatically against the invoice some contra configuration happened in your case. 4) After change the configuration create new invoice Scenario 2 - without cancel original invoice If not possible to cancel invoice due to the legel or any other reason in that case below is the option 1) Use FS00 t code and open GL account remove check box "Post automatically only" 2) Now you are free to enter journal voucher entry to settle the account if account department okay 3) Again fill check box "Post automatically only" in FS00 t code As per my knowledge Credit note is not a solution if you generate a document with reference of the original inovice. If you create a credit note without using a reference document in that case you need to configure a credit note accounting properly. Please inform if you require to change in configuration for invoice or credit note. Hope above solutions are works for you.

entry.title: ABAP CDS extractor on NSDM_E_MCHBH (replacement of MCHBH) in not getting delta in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have created a ABAP CDS delta extractor on NSDM_E_MCHBH (replacement of MCHBH) using CDC. SAP recommends to use replacement if available. When the extractor is used in DataSphere Replication flow, the delta load is failing. 

Error - CDS view is Inconsistent and Definition of the view is too complex for automatic CDC

Note - We are getting warning "Definition of view is too complex for automatic CDS" and "Data extraction of CDS entity not possible" in ABAP perspective. We ignored the warning as it was for other extractor's as well and they are fine.

Note , even extractor on MCHBH we faced the same issue.

Please let me know how we can get delta from table MCHBH using ABAP CDS view extractor.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Migration of Workflow to SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Paul,

Thank you for reaching out to us. I recommend you follow the step-by-step guide below to migrate from Workflow Management to SAP Build Process Automation.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/transition-your-sap-workflow-management-solutions-to-sap-build-process/ba-p/13549045

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/migrate-your-sap-btp-neo-workflow-applications-to-sap-build-process/ba-p/13576528

In case you encounter any issues, please don't hesitate to let us know.

entry.title: Goal-Scoring Therapy: How FIFA on PlayStation Became My Geeky Escape
entry.content: After a week filled with deadlines, unexpected challenges, and the never-ending to-do list, there’s one thing that helps me unwind and find my balance—playing a game of FIFA on my PlayStation. Now, I know what you're thinking: "FIFA? Really?" But let me tell you, this isn’t just a game; it’s my personal escape into a world where the only pressure is making that perfect pass, outsmarting the AI defense, and, of course, celebrating a beautifully executed goal. The minute I turn on the console and hear the familiar crowd noises, it’s like all the stress of the week fades away. I dive into the intricacies of team management, the thrill of competition, and the satisfaction of mastering those tricky skill moves. It’s a world where I’m in control, where my favorite teams and players are at my fingertips, and where every match is a chance to test my strategy and reflexes.

Regards

Rishi

entry.title: How to load multiple files from network SFTP & archive the files by CIDS after sending to IBP.
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

In CI-DS my interfaces is very clear: File to IBP.

As per the requirement,  My interface has to read the data from the SFTP location( MFT as source) and have to archive the files after post-loading the data to IBP. But here the challenge facing as source is in network path and the DS agent/CIDs is in non-network location.

So if the interface made to read the multiple files through the CIDS with multiple data flows, but not able to perform the archiving by this approach.

Please help me on this issue.

Thanks,
Kalyan.

entry.title: Re: Open documents exclude all orders with date 01-01 (al...
entry.content:
hi @jeroenw , 

If you have version 10, you may filter the list of "Open Sales Orders" by "Posting Date" field, using the following syntax:

here i listed 3 years only, but you can apply as many as you have in your DB. 

In general - the analytics and data filtering capabilities of the web client are highly sophisticated and flexible - and at the same time user-friendly

More information is available here:

 https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-sap-business-one-web-client 

regards,

Maya

entry.title: Re: You used to use SE16/SE16N and SQVI, Try SE16H!
entry.content:
Note that the join between BSEG and BUKRS works only in S/4HANA, or in ECC if BSEG has been converted into a transparent table, because BSEG used to be a pooled table in ECC.

An important thing missing here, is how to define the join conditions:

entry.title: Re: Goal-Scoring Therapy: How FIFA on PlayStation Became My Geeky Escape
entry.content:
FIFA has also become a way for me to connect with friends who share the same passion. We talk tactics, share tips, and even have our own mini-tournaments. It’s amazing how something as simple as a game can build such strong connections.

Some might call it a nerdy obsession, but to me, it’s the perfect way to recharge and reset for the week ahead. In those 90 virtual minutes, I find a sense of calm and accomplishment that helps me tackle whatever comes my way next.

So, here’s to the geeky hobbies that bring us joy, connect us with others, and help us keep our sanity. For me, it’s FIFA on PlayStation - my ultimate goal-scoring therapy!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA offering SE16H versus SE11/SE16N/SQVI
entry.content:
Note that the join between BSEG and BUKRS works only in S/4HANA, or in ECC if BSEG has been converted into a transparent table, because BSEG used to be a pooled table in ECC.

An important thing missing here, is how to define the join conditions:

entry.title: Embedding of SAC Stories in SAP Fiori for S/4 HANA on premise
entry.content:
Dear all, 

is it still possible to embedd  SAC Stories (with live data connection to S/4 HANA) into SAP Fiori for S/4 HANA on premise via iframe? I can only find old information on that with links and videos that are partly broken. If yes, could you please provide a link to the documentation? The use case would be to combine the nice look&feel of SAC with the intent-based navigation of Fiori (yes, we have SAC licences). 

BR, Daniel

entry.title: Re: Video Series - Part 8 - Connecting C4C and BTP
entry.content:
I think it's important to explain why one would choose certain types of credentials over others. 

The approach shown here (technical user) should only be used with purely technical background processes like workflow processing. 

For full stack side-by-side extensions which include user interaction the integration with technical credentials cannot be used for security reasons. Instead the OAuthSAMLBearer flow needs to be used to propagate the access rights of the user to the OData access.

It's important to point out which flow to use for which scenario.

Background process: Technical user (ideally with client certificate over basic auth)

Full app with user interaction: OAuthSAMLBearer flow

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
    • First generation college students
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I am passionate about working with SAP, as it plays a crucial role in helping businesses streamline their processes. It’s inspiring to see how technology can have such a significant impact, and I’m eager to continue learning and advancing in this field to build a successful career.

I’m enthusiastic about the opportunity to take a free certification attempt, as it will deepen my knowledge and further enhance my skills. With technology constantly evolving, I’m committed to staying current with the latest developments. I am truly grateful for this opportunity and excited about the future possibilities. Thank you for considering my application for the free certification attempt.

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite Error - ScriptCollectionException -...
entry.content: Hi Willem, The error a ChildCount 4 states the scrip collection Reusable_Groovy_Scripts_V2 are not available, however under Manage Integration Content the scripts have been deployed. Also, in the artifact HomeDepot_COMM_PROCESSDIRECT_SFTP_SENDER for Connection Details the address has been provided - /tpm/b2b/processdirect..

entry.title: Re: How to use DDCDS_CUSTOMER_DOMAIN_VALUE_T?
entry.content: Hello, so the solution is to either assign the same software component for that package. This is because in order for the domain to be seen by your package both of them need to be within same software component. As someone previously mentioned this can be done by using the transaction se80 and then adjusting the software component. Now, if you are using local object for your project or for following the tutorial you can simply create the same GENDER domain by yourself. You just have to click on the local package new and then domain and then you can just copy the same values that are located within your domain that is not accessible because it is located in the different software component.

entry.title: Re: How to Read Domain values using ABAP CDS Entity view
entry.content: Hello if you don't see the data when doing a data preview, the solution is to either assign the same software component for that package. This is because in order for the domain to be seen by your package both of them need to be within same software component. As someone previously mentioned this can be done by using the transaction se80 and then adjusting the software component. Now, if you are using local object for your project or for following the tutorial you can simply create the same GENDER domain by yourself. You just have to click on the local package new and then domain and then you can just copy the same values that are located within your domain that is not accessible because it is located in the different software component.

entry.title: Sales Order to Production Order link query SAP Business One
entry.content:
Dear all,

Need a query related sales order to production order link.

SAP Business One

Regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: How can I use Launchpad Notifications with a RAP App?
entry.content:
Hello Community,

how can I use the Fiori Launchpad Notification with RAP Apps? Is that even planned? The concept behind it is not quite clear to me yet. At least I can't find anything about this in connection with RAP applications.

So far it looks as if the notifications are based on defined workflows. But does a workflow necessarily have to be defined in the SAP system for this? Or can I also use notifications without workflows?

A very simple example:
The user is to be informed that a date has been exceeded for a supplier. (The information for this comes from a simple Fiori element list report based on CDS views.)

Perhaps someone can tell me something about this?

regards

Lars

entry.title: User name for last changer of maintenance plan header is invalid ( API Message MPLAN 106 )
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts, 

When running a custom tcode for maintenance plan mass change error

"User name for last changer of maintenance plan header is invalid ( API Message MPLAN 106 )"

How to resolve this issue

entry.title: Reuse of Calculated Columns in CDS-View
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In thread Reuse of Calculated Columns in CDS View, a solution was posted:

select 
  vbeln,
  netwr,
  mwsbk,
  netwr + mwsbk as total,
  ( netwr + mwsbk ) * 1000 as total1000
from vbrk

Note that there is redundancy here: the expression "netwr + mwsbk" occurs twice. This is OK for such a simple case. However, my calculated fields are much more complex and I would like to reuse existing expressions (already created calculated fields), as:

select 
  vbeln,
  netwr,
  mwsbk,
  netwr + mwsbk as total,
  total * 1000 as total1000
from vbrk

I tried:

  1. Exactly as above: I got error message "The column 'total' is unknown"
  2. With prefix $projection.total: error message "Reuse of $projection expression not possible"
  3. I created a second CDS View which reads from the first one, and then I reused the second as association in the first one. I got error message "Recursive definitions are not supported" (which makes sense)

Are there any ideas?
Thanks!
Igor

entry.title: Re: How to parse an Employee Central ODATA into ABAP table
entry.content: I can retrieve the data in string or json format. I can convert from string to XML using a couple of functions.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content: Done!

entry.title: Re: What is SAP C/4HANA and why should you care?
entry.content:
SAP C/4HANA is an impressive advancement in customer experience (CX) management, particularly for industries that have traditionally struggled with customer satisfaction, like Utilities. At NovelVox, we understand the critical importance of delivering a seamless and integrated CX, especially in sectors where customer interactions can often be complex and multifaceted.

SAP C/4HANA's comprehensive suite of cloud solutions, including data management, marketing, commerce, sales, and service, offers a powerful toolset to transform customer interactions across all touchpoints. By integrating front-office functions with back-office processes via the SAP Cloud Platform and SAP S/4HANA, businesses can ensure a more unified, personalized, and responsive customer journey.

For contact centers, the ability to access complete and contextual customer information in real-time is vital. With solutions like SAP Service Cloud, contact centers can enhance agent efficiency and deliver higher levels of customer satisfaction by providing more accurate and timely responses to customer inquiries. This aligns perfectly with the capabilities we provide at NovelVox, where our focus is on optimizing contact center performance through intelligent integrations and enhanced user interfaces.

entry.title: Re: How to parse an Employee Central ODATA into ABAP table
entry.content:
Sampe code below, but I'm not sure how to get the Odata into a table listing the fields and values:

lv_uri = |/odata/v2/BenefitEnrollment?&$filter=id eq '90000001_DENT'&fromDate=1800-01-01&toDate=9999-12-31|. 

    cl_http_utility=>set_request_uri( request = go_http_client->request
                                      uri = lv_uri ).

go_http_client->send( EXCEPTIONS http_communication_failure = 1 http_invalid_state = 2 ).

go_http_client->receive( EXCEPTIONS http_communication_failure = 1 http_invalid_state = 2 http_processing_failed = 3 ).

CLEAR lv_cdata.

lv_cdata = go_http_client->response->get_cdata( ).

CLEAR lv_xdata.

CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_STRING_TO_XSTRING' EXPORTING text = lv_cdata IMPORTING buffer = lv_xdata EXCEPTIONS failed = 1 OTHERS = 2.

CHECK sy-subrc = 0 .

CLEAR: lt_xml, gt_ret2.

CALL FUNCTION 'SMUM_XML_PARSE' EXPORTING xml_input = lv_xdata TABLES xml_table = lt_xml return = gt_ret2.

REFRESH gt_xml.

APPEND LINES OF lt_xml TO gt_xml.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content:
Here my solution for week 4:

Thank you for this great challenge! Had a lot of fun with these exercises. 

Greetings

Sebastian

entry.title: Empowering Your Journey with SAP Cloud ALM and Clean Core Mindset
entry.content:
Unleashing the Value of SAP Cloud ALM for S/4HANA Cloud with Clean Core

In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, managing your RISE with SAP implementation projects requires not just technical expertise but also a strategic approach that ensures sustainable growth and customer satisfaction. SAP Cloud ALM, when combined with a clean core mindset, provides the necessary tools and methodologies to streamline your implementation projects, optimize operations, and ultimately accelerate the sales cycle. We're excited to introduce our new learning journey designed to equip you with these essential skills.

Why This Learning Journey Matters

Unlock New Capabilities: The learning journey, Unleashing the Value of SAP Cloud ALM for S/4HANA Cloud with Clean Core, is specifically crafted to help you gain a comprehensive understanding of SAP Cloud ALM. This knowledge is vital for deploying, adopting, and extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition while adhering to the clean core principles that prevent unnecessary customizations.

Accelerate Your Sales Cycle: By positioning SAP Cloud ALM effectively within your teams—whether in Sales, Presales, Customer Engagement, or Technical Quality Management—you can streamline project execution, minimize complexity, and deliver faster time-to-value. This not only boosts adoption but also drives customer satisfaction, setting the stage for long-term success.

Ensure Operational Excellence: Post go-live, SAP Cloud ALM becomes your go-to solution for managing operational tasks and monitoring the health of your systems. This ensures that your SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment remains robust, secure, and aligned with business objectives.

Key Learning Objectives

After completing this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Discover the Value of SAP Cloud ALM: Understand its full range of capabilities and how they align with your implementation strategies.
  • Position SAP Cloud ALM Effectively: Learn how to articulate the benefits of SAP Cloud ALM to various stakeholders, including Sales, Presales, Customer Engagement Executives (CEEs), Cloud Solution Partners (CSPs), and Technical Quality Managers (TQMs).
  • Empower Your Organization: Equip your teams with the knowledge to maintain clean core systems, reducing the burden of customizations and ensuring a more agile and adaptable IT landscape.
  • Unlock the Full Potential of SAP Solutions: Leverage SAP Cloud ALM not only for SAP S/4HANA Cloud but across your entire SAP ecosystem to drive innovation and operational excellence.

Who Should Enroll?

This learning journey is designed for a broad audience within SAP, including Administrators, Architects, Business Users, CEEs, Consultants, Presales, Project Managers, Sales, and Support Consultants. Whether you are a beginner looking to build a foundational understanding or a professional seeking to expand your expertise, this learning journey is tailored to meet your needs.

No Prerequisites Required

This learning journey is open to everyone, with no prior experience necessary. It’s an excellent starting point for anyone interested in mastering SAP Cloud ALM and understanding its impact on the clean core strategy.

Begin Your Journey Today

Embark on this learning journey and empower yourself with the tools and knowledge to excel in your role. By mastering SAP Cloud ALM and embracing a clean core mindset, you’ll be better equipped to drive your projects to success, accelerate the sales cycle, and create happier customers.

Get started now and Unleashing Value of SAP Cloud ALM for S/4HANA Cloud with Clean Core

entry.title: How can I retrieve the displayed value of a field like `KNA1-LIFNR`, or in cases like `DFKKBPTAXNUM`
entry.content: I need to fetch the value that is currently shown on the screen (in the form) for a single field, such as `KNA1-LIFNR`, or multiple values from a field like `DFKKBPTAXNUM`, rather than the value stored in the database. This is for validation purposes within an SAP method. What is the best way to obtain these displayed values?

entry.title: Re: New Installation of S/4HANA 1610FPS2 - Part 3 - Best Practices Content Activation
entry.content:
Can the link in 29 be reactivated please? I'm unable to access. 

Thanks,

Ganesh

entry.title: Re: Receiving "Destination Host is unreachable!" error when executing SAC stories using HA
entry.content:
To investigate connectivity issue I would always check the Chrome Developer Tools.

These can be accessed via View -> Developer -> Developer Tools or F12.
Select the Network Tab. 
Now open the SAC story and look out for non 200 status responses.
Select that row and you will see the response which should give an indication as to the issue.

entry.title: Re: Migrating Applications from XSJS to Async-XSJS
entry.content:
Hi VolkerSaggau,

we have another problem related to async-xsjs after switching to SAP HANA CLOUD.
In practice, the connection parameter is no longer valued within the custom CRUD functions, below is the code used:

1)XSODATA:

"DB_CALCULATIONVIEWS_PACKAGEONLY" as "PackageOnly" key ("ID") create using "xsjs.crud:package.xsjslib::insertCrudPackage";

2) FUNCTION:

async function insertCrudPackage(param) {
    var after = param.afterTableName;
   var pstmt = await param.connection.prepareStatement("select * from \"" + after.toUpperCase() + "\"");
    var oTable = await SESSIONINFO.recordSetToJSON(await pstmt.executeQuery(), "Details");

In console we saw that param.connection is null while param.afterTableName is valorized.

The Error showed is:

{"code":"500","message":{"lang":"en-US","value":"Internal Server Error"},"innererror":"invalid table name:  Could not find table/view #NEW_PACKAGEONLY1_17_1_1 in schema DWMHC: line 1 col 15 (at pos 14)"}} 

Can you suggest something?

Thanks

entry.title: Hello SAP Guru, I have set Up Scope of List as ALV in ME57 in customization but its not working
entry.content:
I have set Up Scope of List as ALV in ME57 in customization but its not working while running ME57, System is taking default as "A"

Please help how can i set to default to ALV, i ma using SAP S4HANA 2023 version.

entry.title: Re: Batch Load Data into a Custom Business Object in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Everything is working great except if custom business objects has any field with data types Quantity/Amount is not working while uploading data through excel.

@MartyMcCormick

entry.title: SAP CAP How to test DESTINATION to S4/Hana on premise within BAS PRIOR to deploy?
entry.content:
Hej guys,
I read (felt) every tutorial about that but it seems that I am too dumb to get the final clue, so if someone knows the answer on a level "for dummies" I'd really appreciate...!!

How can I use the approuter or other mechanism to test a CAP App - which should consume a DESTINATION LOCALLY within Business Appplication Studio (BAS) - , WITHOUT deploying the whole thing to BTP PRIOR TO TESTING???

  • Simple CAP App (w/o Fiori elements)
  • Destination in BTP is defined,
  • There is a connection from BAS to DESTINATION, successfully checked with “curl “destination.dest/…path/service…”
  • I need just ONE but WORKING example

    Do I need to programmatically get the JSON Web Token (JWT) then HOW!!??!

    thanks for any answer...
    nearly desperated---- ;-(  -----

entry.title: Re: UDF Setup history item list BP ordered
entry.content:
Dear Lothar,
Unfortunately it doesn't work. 

The query adept itself and gives a failure

Please advise.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Migrating Workflows to Build Process Automation
entry.content:
After solving an incident ticket with a representative from the Collaboration (PLM-CLOUD-COL) team, i can affirm that doing this DOES NOT allow you to import/edit the old workflows inside BPA. As a confirmation, the representative mentioned they are still using the Business Application Studio for modifying or enhancing the old workflows.

Therefore if you intend on using BPA as the editing software, you have to recreate the entire process inside BPA from the beginning.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP How to test DESTINATION to S4/Hana on premise within BAS PRIOR to deploy?
entry.content: whatever Information you need just ask, I am a nube so I didn't want to spam you with unnecessary information

entry.title: "Optimizing SAP Landscape: The Power of Data Archiving for Performance and Compliance."
entry.content:
Introduction:

In the modern business environment, data is both a powerful asset and a significant challenge. With the exponential growth of data, organizations are increasingly confronted with the need to manage this resource effectively. SAP Data Archiving emerges as a strategic solution, allowing companies to optimize system performance, ensure compliance, and control operational costs by systematically managing data growth.

Understanding SAP Data Archiving :

SAP Data Archiving involves the transfer of historical data that is no longer actively used from the primary database to a separate, secure storage. This process is crucial for maintaining the efficiency of SAP systems, reducing database size, and ensuring that data management aligns with organizational objectives and regulatory requirements.

The Strategic Importance of SAP Data Archiving

 Enhancing System Performance: As databases expand over time, the increased volume of data can lead to slower transaction processing and reporting times, which can hinder business operations. By archiving data that is no longer in active use, organizations can streamline their databases, resulting in improved performance, faster access to critical information, and more efficient system operations.

 Ensuring Compliance and Legal Adherence: Various industries are subject to stringent data retention laws that dictate how long certain types of data must be stored. SAP Data Archiving ensures that businesses comply with these legal requirements by retaining necessary data in a secure and accessible manner, thus minimizing the risk of non-compliance and associated penalties.

Optimizing Storage Costs: The cost of data storage can escalate quickly as the volume of data grows. By moving obsolete data to cost-effective storage solutions, SAP Data Archiving helps organizations reduce the need for expensive, high-performance storage, leading to significant cost savings. This approach also frees up valuable space within the primary database, further optimizing storage resources.

The SAP Data Archiving Procedure Using SARA

The SAP SARA (Archive Administration) transaction is central to the archiving process in SAP. It offers a structured approach for managing the life cycle of data archiving. Here’s a detailed look at the steps involved:

Transaction:

 SARA -      Archive Administration

 

 Selecting the Archiving Object:

In the SARA screen, the first step is to select the archiving object relevant to the data you wish to archive. An archiving object represents specific data types or tables in SAP. For example, WORKITEM is an archiving object used for archiving workflow items and work items. Selecting the correct archiving object is crucial, as it dictates what data will be archived.

Before proceeding, you may need to customize the Archive Administration to fit your specific needs. This involves configuring the Logical File Path settings.

 

 

 Perform the Logical File Path Definition

 

  Pre-Processing:

Depending on the archiving object, you might need to perform pre-processing activities. These tasks prepare the data for archiving by checking its integrity and ensuring it meets the archiving criteria. Some archiving objects have built-in pre-processing steps that must be completed before moving forward.

 

Creating a New Archiving Session:

In the SARA screen, create a new archiving session by selecting the Write button. This action generates the archive files where the data will be stored. You will be prompted to provide details such as the session name, variant, and any specific parameters for the archiving run.

Executing the Archiving Session:

Once the session is set up, execute the archiving process by running the session. The system transfers the data from the primary database to the archive files. During this process, the data is temporarily held in the archive files before being moved to the final storage location. The progress and status of the archiving session can be monitored in real-time.

 Data Deletion:

Following a successful archiving session, the next step is to delete the archived data from the primary database. This deletion is managed through the Delete function in the SARA transaction. It’s crucial to confirm that the data has been securely archived and is retrievable before deletion. This step effectively reduces the size of the primary database, enhancing system performance.

 

Managing Archived Data:

Archived data is stored in designated storage systems, which can be SAP ILM or third-party solutions. Managing this data includes ensuring its security, accessibility, and compliance with retention policies. Tools within SAP allow for browsing and retrieving archived data when needed, ensuring that archived information remains available for audits, reporting, or legal inquiries.

Data Access and Retrieval:

Even after data is archived, it remains accessible for future use. SAP provides tools such as SARJ (Archive Explorer) to browse and retrieve archived data. This functionality ensures that while the data is no longer in the primary database, it can still be accessed as required.

Best Practices for Effective SAP Data Archiving

To fully realize the benefits of SAP Data Archiving, consider the following best practices:

  1. Establish a Regular Archiving Schedule: Implementing a routine archiving process helps prevent the accumulation of outdated data, ensuring that your database remains optimized for performance.
  2. Align Archiving with Data Retention Policies: Ensure that your archiving strategy is tailored to meet the organization’s data retention requirements. This alignment is crucial for both compliance and efficient data management.
  3. Thorough Testing and Validation: Before finalizing the deletion of data from the primary database, ensure that the data has been accurately archived and can be retrieved if needed. Conduct comprehensive testing to mitigate the risk of data loss.
  4. Continuous Monitoring and Reporting: Regularly monitor the archiving process and generate reports to assess its effectiveness. This practice allows for timely adjustments and ensures that the archiving strategy remains aligned with evolving business needs.
  5. Invest in User Training: Educating users about the archiving process and how to access archived data is essential for minimizing disruptions and ensuring smooth operations. Proper training helps users understand the importance of archiving and facilitates easier data retrieval.

Conclusion

SAP Data Archiving is a critical component of any robust data management strategy. By systematically managing data growth, organizations can enhance system performance, ensure compliance with legal requirements, and reduce operational costs. Adopting best practices in SAP Data Archiving not only safeguards data integrity but also supports long-term business objectives by optimizing resources and improving overall efficiency.

entry.title: Re: My Fiori Elements Story – Part 2
entry.content:
We no longer recommend creating an additional parameter for the tile/target mapping, like sap-appvar-id or save-appvar-id. If you created such a parameter in a previous support package, you can continue using it.
Instead we recommend to use unique semantic object/action, see chapter "Settings for SAPUI5 App Variants (created via key user adaptation or an adaptation project)": https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/33daedef95454af68903ef1238aa0373.html?version=latest#loio33daedef95454af68903ef1238aa0373__appvariants

Best regards
Silke

entry.title: Re: How to diagnose issue with sap-appvar-id for Deployed Adaptation Project?
entry.content: FYI - We no longer recommend creating an additional parameter for the tile/target mapping, like sap-appvar-id or save-appvar-id. If you created such a parameter in a previous support package, you can continue using it. Instead we recommend to use unique semantic object/action, see chapter "Settings for SAPUI5 App Variants (created via key user adaptation or an adaptation project)": https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/33daedef95454af68903ef1238aa0373.html?version=latest#loio33daedef95454af68903ef1238aa0373__appvariants Best regards Silke

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi,

Label is picked either from the annotation where you specify the label or from the data element. Check the data element of both the fields.

CORS are good enough and it is working fine with other reports and blocking few set of reports.

entry.title: How to identify which users has execute access on a function in iq
entry.content:
How to identify which users has execute access on a function in iq

Q: Detail the IQ environment: exact IQ server version, OS version.
A: SP05 PL10


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Failed to execute 'send' on 'XMLHttpRequest'
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm working on screen personas and I want to apply an example code to get some extra data on page.

I modify this code and get error. In my opinion I missing the credentials and authentitacion but how to improve it?

 

if (session.idExists('wnd[0]/usr/txtPersonas_168387506699773')) {
if (session.findById('wnd[0]/usr/txtPersonas_168387506699773').text != '') {
var altID = session.findById('wnd[0]/usr/txtPersonas_168387506699773').text;
session.utils.log(altID);
//Create the request object
var http = new XMLHttpRequest();
//Specify the type of request, the url and if the request should be handled
asynchronously or not
http.open(
'GET',
"https://xxxx.xxx.xxx.xxxx/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_handlingunit/srvd_a2x/sap/handlingunit/0001/HandlingUn
itAlternativeID?$filter=Warehouse eq ‘1050’ and EWMHndlgUnitAltvID eq ‘” +
altID +“’”,
false
);
//set request header attributes
http.setRequestHeader(‘DataServiceVersion’, ‘2.0’);
http.setRequestHeader(‘Accept’, ‘application/json’);
//Send the request
http.send();
//Process the response
if (http.readyState == 4 && http.status == 200) {
//The response is available in the http.response property
session.utils.log(http.response);
try {
var HU = JSON.parse(http.response).value[0].HandlingUnitExternalID;
session.utils.log(HU);

session.findById(‘wnd[0]/usr/subX:/SCWM/SAPLRF_INQUIRY_PM:0200/txt/SCWM/S_RF
_SELECTION-RFHU’).text = HU;
} catch (error) {
session.utils.log(‘HU number could not be determined based on alternative HU
ID ‘ + altID);
session.utils.alert(‘HU number could not be determined based on alternative
HU ID ‘ + altID);
}
} else {
session.utils.log(‘OData API query failed’);
session.utils.alert(‘Odata API query failed’);
}
session.utils.log(‘API call finished’);
} else {
session.utils.log(‘API NOT called’);
}
}

entry.title: Re: How to identify which users has execute access on a f...

entry.title: Re: webide doesn't import full app from ABAP repository
entry.content: Check the log in your webide.

entry.title: Developing locally with PostgreSQL BTP Instance
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to run my application locally with data from a deployed BTP PostgreSQL instance. I am successful in running an SSH tunnel and connecting to the DB via DBeaver.

However when I adjust the settings in my CAP Application, I do see during startup that the settings are taken into account correctly.

However when the application is launched I get an immediate 'Timeout'.

Would anyone be able to assist me? 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Convergent Charging: Recurring Charge not triggered f...
entry.content: Thanks for the swift response Eric.

entry.title: SAC - Calculated Measure
entry.content:
Hello community

I have made the following calculated measure to calculated fixed and variable costs. The logic is that if the property of the dimension "Cost_Unit"  is "Var" (variable) then it should multiply "Value" with "Avg_Participants":

However, it doesn't work properly. It seems that it cannot do the multiplication when the Cost_Unit is variable. I suppose it is because "Avg_Participants" is stored on the dimension members on "Course" and "#" on the dimension "Cost_Unit".

Is there any workaround to make it read "Avg_Participants" properly?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: In FBCJ only 1 partcular profit center to be accepted for 1 cash journal.
entry.content:
Dear esteemed members,

There is a requirement that when transactions are posted using FBCJ for a particular cash journal, it should allow only a particular profit center. If any other profit center is selected, the system should throw an error.

For eg.: Cash journal HG01 should only accept Profit Center 1000. Currently, the user can enter any profit center from the list. If any other profit center is selected (apart from 1000) the system should throw an error. How to achieve this? Kindly guide. Thanks in advance. Appreciate all the help!

entry.title: Personal setting "company code" in Me21n
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I need to make insivible by default the personnal setting "company code" in Me21n.

I tried by SHD0 with the option "Invisible" but my test is KO.
I did the variante's activation and user's affectation.
Do you have any idea why my test with SHD0 is KO or maybe an other idea in SPRO?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
Yes, that's right.

Cause i want to do it automatically, and user don't need to run a programm every time, he wants to get a document. 

document are uploading to portal during the day.

Other companies uploads documents to portal, which mine company need to get.

There is a opportunity : one of the componets allow to download a document since the input date(timestamp) to current dare.

And i will get all the documents, which was uploaded at this interval.

So i want, to declare a date (like 30 minutes ago), and put it to parameter of the componet. And run a program every 30 minutes.

In this case user don't need to run a programm, and all the documents will have  been downloading automatically.

entry.title: Customer Standard Return - Returns Overview - Returns Steps - Transfer To Scrap - CDS View #COE
entry.content:
In the Manage Customer Returns – Version 2 app there is a Returns Overview section for each of the return order items that provides the return steps. The first level in this example is the customer return with shipment to plant. This first level step includes the following steps: returns order, returns delivery, goods receipt, inspection, logistical follow-up, credit memo request, goods movement for valuation and credit memo. The next first level step in the example is transfer to scrap with the next level step below that being goods movement to scrap. 

We are trying to develop a report that uses either a CDS View or an API that will provide this information. The main issue that we are encountering is that the goods movement to scrap return step under transfer to scrap does not appear to be related to the return order itself. When I view the material document information for the goods movement to scrap transaction it does not contain the return order. Please advise how this relationship can be determined and provide the CDS Views or APIs that can be utilized to recreate the Return Steps that appear under the Returns Overview section of the Manage Customer Returns – Version 2 app. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations on your S
entry.content:
Hi @hariprasadgud , 

Thank you for the confirmation that ONB2.0 is not yet supported. Is there a roadmap date for it?

We are currently leveraging the Instance Sync and we would like to know if we should plan to do cutovers manually once Instance Sync is deprecated. 

Best,

Camélia

entry.title: Can Analytical Queries be used in AFO of SAC Add-In?
entry.content: Can Analytical queries (S4 Public Cloud) be used in Excel based reporting via either the Excel Add-In or AFO edition for SAC?

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS Release News 2408 – External Entities
entry.content: Any plans to offer this in the ABAP Cloud Environment in S/4HANA Private Cloud and/or On Premise?

entry.title: Re: Embracing Sustainability with SAP Ariba
entry.content:
For inputs on Sustainable Procurement with reference to BRSR (Business Responsibility and Sustainability Reporting) in India, kindly refer this interesting article.
Impact of Sustainable Procurement in ESG

entry.title: Same LTMC Staging table throughout the SAP Environments
entry.content:
Hello,

Did anyone across a scenario where we have multiple SAP systems and we have to work with LTMC staging tables. The issue is the name of staging tables get changed in another sap system for same object.

Because of the different table name, we will be having difficulties manages the tables in BODS. Because if we want load data in another sap system, we will need to change the table. For example if we have 10 SAP systems then 10 different staging tables for the same object and we will have to manage those 10 tables each time we execute the BODS job.

Can anyone please explain if you have come across this scenario.

Thanks,

Imran

Regards,

govindu uday

entry.title: How to keep track of graduates in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We want to track our graduates in SuccessFactors. Who are they, how long do they work in our company, which positions do they get after being a graduate and so on. As far as I know, there aren't any standard solutions for this. 

Any recommendations? 

Br,

Ines

entry.title: Re: In FBCJ only 1 partcular profit center to be accepted...
entry.content: Perform first some search on FI validation rule

entry.title: Bulk Download Onboarding 1.0 Documents
entry.content: What is the best way to extract all documents out of ONB 1.0? We have enabled the bulk download feature but the zip files are not user friendly as you don't know who the new hire is until you open the zip file and look at the files. It appears we are unable to export all files if we do an unfiltered search and bulk download to SFTP.  I looked at using postman to utilize the API but we are limited to 100 files per pull. Looking for recommendations on how to best export all documents for all new hires.

entry.title: Error oData Iflow in CPI - java.util.ArrayList cannot be cast to java.util.HashMap
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have an iflow with oData service in CPI, and when consume with Postman, I have the following error when the response returns multilple records:

java.lang.ClassCastException: java.util.ArrayList (loaded by <bootstrap>) cannot be cast to java.util.HashMap (loaded by <bootstrap>)

Any suggest what may be happening?

Attached EDMX model and xml response.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Recalculation of header conditions when cancelling a...
entry.content: Hello Raymond, thank you for your reply. yes we actually saw that but did not find what we needed to. We actually found a solution. The key is to enhance the cancellation reasons with an "X" value at Statistics.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for the submission. For better readability kindly display the text along with criticality

entry.title: Re: Building an ABAP RESTful Application
entry.content: I have tried the above and it is actually working the error for my case Field for creation user is missing in source reference Z40_GROCERY type TABL. when i want to generate ABAP repository object has been resolved. Thanks alot

entry.title: Re: Packaging Specification in Embedded EWM
entry.content:
PLEASE CHECK whether the following configuration has been maintained in your system

SPRO-> SAP Extended Warehouse Management -> Goods Receipt process -> Inbound delivery -> Existence check of packaging specification -> Activate Existence Check of Packaging Specification.
